title: Concurrent Cache Access, Duplicate Suppression, Ownership, and Race Evidence · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A volunteer expedition training program and PokéAPI service steward. They need repeatable list and detail exploration with canonical resource keys, TTL and capacity limits, duplicate suppression, independent cancellation, API-friendly traffic, and race evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Fair-Use Expedition Browser and Shared Cache with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Cache every requested resource, limit frequency and concurrency, identify the client responsibly, and avoid unsupported availability claims.
Inject and reuse one configured http.Client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use.
Canonicalize endpoint, resource identity, and representation into a typed cache key without mixing user spelling or presentation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Pokedex Go Fair Use Policy
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go team handles case 3759 during a clean cross-platform package smoke. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove duplicate suppression, independent waiters, safe publication, and in-flight cleanup; browser code must remain deterministic and side-effect-free; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Cache every requested resource, limit frequency and concurrency, identify the client responsibly, and avoid unsupported availability claims.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Pokedex Go Fair Use Policy.

project case — A project Go team handles case 9662 during a narrow-terminal ranger workflow. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove duplicate suppression, independent waiters, safe publication, and in-flight cleanup; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use barriers, controlled transports, many interleavings, race runs, duplicate-count assertions, and leak-sensitive cleanup checks.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Project Pokedex Go Concurrency Tes….

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Pokedex Go Concurrency Test Evidence happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Pokedex Go Concurrency…
steps.instruction:
Write the project evidence note for Pokedex Go Concurrency Test Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A project Go team handles case 9471 during a clean cross-platform package smoke. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove duplicate suppression, independent waiters, safe publication, and in-flight cleanup; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use traffic budget must remain enforced; then change one resource identity, pagination link, JSON field, status, or body budget and revalidate admission. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use barriers, controlled transports, many interleavings, race runs, duplicate-count assertions, and leak-sensitive cleanup checks.
